prompt:
A MeridianOps Retail Performance platform capable of running entirely on an on-premise laptop or in-store server enables store managers to coordinate promotions, staff training, inventory, and workforce timekeeping from a single Vue.js web interface in English. Administrators configure marketing campaigns such as percent-off discounts, fixed-amount coupons, and full-reduction offers (for example, “$10 off $50”); each campaign has an effective date range, a daily redemption cap (default 200), and per-member limits (default 1 per day), with issuance via printable QR coupons or account-based assignment and redemption at checkout by scanning. Member accounts support tiers (Base/Silver/Gold) and points accrual (1 point per $1 pre-tax, rounded down) with optional stored-value balances for internal credit, all visible to authorized cashiers and managers with clear redemption feedback and an audit trail view. For staff development, supervisors publish short quizzes tied to products and policies; the system builds each employee’s daily review queue using a spaced-repetition schedule, lets supervisors weight topics by difficulty, and shows explainable recommendations such as “review in 3 days due to two recent misses,” including hit-rate statistics and trend lines. Floor leads handle inventory through guided screens for inbound receiving, outbound transfers, and stock counts, with batch and expiration date capture when enabled, and real-time indicators when stock is reserved for open orders. Timekeeping supports QR/NFC check-in/out with optional GPS coordinate capture (no maps required), configurable tolerance (default ±5 minutes), and anti-fraud rules like device binding and one-time QR codes rotating every 30 seconds on the local server display; employees can submit make-up requests that managers approve with notes. Regional dashboards and ad-hoc visualizations are built by dragging components into layouts, linking filters, drilling down by store and date, exporting to PNG/PDF/CSV, and sharing read-only links on the local network.
The backend uses FastAPI to expose decoupled REST-style endpoints consumed by the Vue.js SPA, enforcing validation, permissions, and transactional consistency, with PostgreSQL as the sole system of record for offline persistence. Roles include Administrator (global configuration and access control), Store Manager (approvals, dashboards, campaign execution), Inventory Clerk (documents and counts), Cashier (redemption and member lookup), and Employee (training and attendance); permissions scope what data appears in reports and shared links. Inventory accuracy is guaranteed by an append-only inventory ledger and document workflow: receiving/counting posts adjustments, orders create reservations, and cancellations or fulfillment release reservations, all executed in database transactions with row-level locking to prevent double-spend of stock. Attendance calculations apply configurable rules such as cross-day shifts, automatic break deductions after 6 hours (default 30 minutes), and late/early penalties (default 0.25 hours per incident beyond tolerance), with exports designed for payroll reconciliation. Campaign performance is retained with traceability by storing issuance/redemption events, operator identity, and timestamps, and scheduled statistics jobs run locally (for example, nightly at 2:00 AM) to materialize KPIs like conversion rate, average order value, and inventory turnover without any external services. Security is fully local: username/password authentication only, minimum 12-character passwords with bcrypt hashing, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, optional at-rest encryption for stored-value and personally identifying fields, and masking of sensitive values in logs and exports by default.
